conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child named Alex who is 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to ask some additional questions as Child Benefit typically has age restrictions.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is Alex enrolled in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished their studies a while back.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Let me read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the official guidance I've just reviewed, I can now provide you with a determination regarding your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Eligibility Decision: Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason is:** Child Benefit is available for children under 16 years old. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 **and** they're in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in education or training, they exceed the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

Child Benefit eligibility ends when a young person either:
- Turns 20 (if they were in approved education or training)
- Leaves approved education or training after age 16
- Reaches age 16 if they're not continuing in education or training

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the UK government's Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if the child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not in any education or training, they exceed the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Alex does not meet the age criteria as they are over 20 years old, and therefore you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in education or training, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if the child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not in any education or training, they exceed the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Alex does not meet the age criteria as they are over 20 years old, and therefore you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in education or training, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
